ip_outcome_note: Confirmed Charmander: small orange lizard with flame-tipped tail matches sm3-18 record. National Pokedex no. 4, Fire-type starter. Illustrated by Kagemaru Himeno.

trivia_bullets.text: Illustrated by Kagemaru Himeno, who has created Pokemon TCG art since the Jungle set (1996 JP, 1999 EN) and has illustrated 654+ cards as of 2024 — one of the longest-tenured illustrators in the franchise. Works in watercolor with bright highlights and characteristic white flecks.

This BUS-18 print is a distinct artwork from VIV-023 Charmander already in the corpus (also Kagemaru Himeno). Two Himeno Charmander prints across two different TCG eras (Sun & Moon vs. Sword & Shield).

"The flame on its tail indicates Charmander's life force. If it is healthy, the flame burns brightly." One of the most iconic pieces of Generation I lore — the implication that a dying flame means a dying Charmander became widely referenced across Pokedex entries and the anime.

Burning Shadows (SM3), released August 5, 2017. Charmander is a Kanto starter common in a set thematically focused on Alolan legendaries — straightforward Pokedex representation carrying the charizard-line cohort into the SM block.

related_cards.name: Charmander
related_cards.note: Alternate Himeno print already in corpus (VIV-023)
